197	    def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
198	        if not self.grammars:
199	            self.vocab_mask = None
200	            self.apply_mask_func = None
201	            return
202	
203	        # Find a grammar from the list
204	        first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
205	
206	        # TODO(lianmin): Maybe we can reuse the existing mask?
207	        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
208	            vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
209	            batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
210	            device=self.device,
211	        )
212	        self.apply_mask_func = (
213	            first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
214	        )  # force to use static method
215	
216	        # Apply the mask
217	        for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
218	            if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
219	                grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
220	
221	        # Move the mask to the device if needed
222	        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)
223	
224	    def update_penalties(self):
225	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
226	            self.acc_linear_penalties = torch.zeros(
227	                (len(self.temperatures), self.vocab_size),
228	                dtype=torch.float32,
229	                device=self.temperatures.device,
230	            )
231	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(self.acc_linear_penalties)
232	        else:
233	            self.acc_linear_penalties = None
234	
235	    def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
236	        if self.acc_linear_penalties is not None:
237	            # Used in the overlap mode
238	            logits.add_(self.acc_linear_penalties)
239	
240	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator and self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
241	            # Used in the non-overlap mode
242	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(logits)
243	
244	        if self.vocab_mask is not None:
245	            self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
246	
247	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
248	            logits.add_(self.logit_bias)
249	
250	    def filter_batch(self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor):
251	        self.penalizer_orchestrator.filter(keep_indices_device)
252	
253	        if self.has_custom_logit_processor:
254	            self._filter_batch_custom_logit_processor(keep_indices, keep_indices_device)
255	
256	        for item in [
257	            "temperatures",
258	            "top_ps",
259	            "top_ks",
260	            "min_ps",
261	            "sampling_seed",
262	        ]:
263	            value = getattr(self, item, None)
264	            if value is not None:
265	                setattr(self, item, value[keep_indices_device])
266	
267	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
268	            self.logit_bias = self.logit_bias[keep_indices_device]
269	
270	    def _filter_batch_custom_logit_processor(
271	        self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor
272	    ):
273	        """Filter the custom logit processor and custom params"""
274	        self.custom_logit_processor = {
275	            k: (p, mask[keep_indices_device])
276	            for k, (p, mask) in self.custom_logit_processor.items()
277	            if torch.any(
278	                mask[keep_indices_device]
279	            )  # ignore the custom logit processor whose mask is all False
280	        }
281	        self.custom_params = [self.custom_params[i] for i in keep_indices]
282	
283	        # If the custom logit processor is an empty dict, set the flag to False,
284	        # and set the custom logit processor and custom params to None.
285	        if len(self.custom_logit_processor) == 0:
286	            self.custom_logit_processor = None
287	            self.custom_params = None
288	            self.has_custom_logit_processor = False
289	
290	    @staticmethod
291	    def merge_custom_logit_processor(
292	        lhs: Optional[Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]],
293	        rhs: Optional[Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]],
294	        bs1: int,
295	        bs2: int,
296	        device: str,
297	    ):
298	        if lhs is None and rhs is None:
299	            return None
300	        lhs, rhs = lhs or {}, rhs or {}
301	
302	        keys = set(lhs.keys()).union(set(rhs.keys()))
303	        merged_dict = {}
304	
305	        for k in keys:
306	            # Get the logit processor object
307	            processor = lhs[k][0] if k in lhs else rhs[k][0]
308	            # Get and merge the mask tensors from the two dicts
309	            left_mask = (
310	                lhs[k][1]
311	                if k in lhs
312	                else torch.zeros(bs1, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
313	            )
314	            right_mask = (
315	                rhs[k][1]
316	                if k in rhs
317	                else torch.zeros(bs2, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
318	            )
319	            merged_dict[k] = (processor, torch.cat([left_mask, right_mask]))
320	
321	            assert merged_dict[k][1].shape[0] == bs1 + bs2, (
322	                f"The batch size of merged mask ({merged_dict[k][1].shape[0]}) does not match "
323	                f"the sum of the batch sizes of the two masks ({bs1 + bs2})"
324	                f"\n{left_mask=}\n{right_mask=}\n{bs1=}\n{bs2=}"
325	                f"\n{lhs=}\n{rhs=}"
326	            )
327	
328	        return merged_dict
329	
330	    def merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
331	        self.penalizer_orchestrator.merge(other.penalizer_orchestrator)
332	
333	        # Merge the custom logit processors and custom params lists
334	        if self.has_custom_logit_processor or other.has_custom_logit_processor:
335	            # Merge the custom logit processors
336	            self.custom_logit_processor = (
337	                SamplingBatchInfo.merge_custom_logit_processor(
338	                    self.custom_logit_processor,
339	                    other.custom_logit_processor,
340	                    len(self),
341	                    len(other),
342	                    self.device,
343	                )
344	            )
345	            # Merge the custom params lists
346	            self.custom_params = self.custom_params or [None] * len(self)
347	            other.custom_params = other.custom_params or [None] * len(other)
348	            self.custom_params.extend(other.custom_params)
349	
350	            # Set the flag to True if any of the two has custom logit processor
351	            self.has_custom_logit_processor = True
352	
353	        # Merge logit bias - note this has to come before the temperatures tensor update! Otherwise will cause crashes.
354	        # See note below on len(self) and len(other).
355	        self.logit_bias = merge_bias_tensor(
356	            self.logit_bias, other.logit_bias, len(self), len(other), self.device, 0.0
357	        )
358	
359	        # Note: because the __len()__ operator is defined on the temperatures tensor,
360	        # please make sure any merge operation with len(self) or len(other) is done before
361	        # the merge operation of the temperatures tensor below.
362	        for item in [
363	            "temperatures",
364	            "top_ps",
365	            "top_ks",
366	            "min_ps",
367	            "sampling_seed",
368	        ]:
369	            self_val = getattr(self, item, None)
370	            other_val = getattr(other, item, None)

377	        self.need_min_p_sampling |= other.need_min_p_sampling

387	def merge_bias_tensor(
388	    lhs: Optional[torch.Tensor],
389	    rhs: Optional[torch.Tensor],
390	    bs1: int,
391	    bs2: int,
392	    device: str,
393	    default: float,
394	):
395	    """Merge two bias tensors for batch merging.
396	
397	    Args:
398	        lhs: Left-hand side tensor
399	        rhs: Right-hand side tensor
400	        bs1: Batch size of left-hand side tensor
401	        bs2: Batch size of right-hand side tensor
402	        device: Device to place the merged tensor on
403	        default: Default value for missing tensor elements
404	
405	    Returns:
406	        Merged tensor or None if both inputs are None
407	    """
408	    if lhs is None and rhs is None:
409	        return None
410	
411	    if lhs is not None and rhs is not None:
412	        return torch.cat([lhs, rhs])
413	    else:
414	        if lhs is not None:
415	            shape, dtype = lhs.shape[1:], lhs.dtype
416	        else:
417	            shape, dtype = rhs.shape[1:], rhs.dtype
418	
419	        if lhs is None:
420	            lhs = torch.empty((bs1, *shape), device=device, dtype=dtype).fill_(default)
421	        if rhs is None:
422	            rhs = torch.empty((bs2, *shape), device=device, dtype=dtype).fill_(default)
423	        return torch.cat([lhs, rhs])

4	def is_legacy_structural_tag(obj: Dict) -> bool:
5	    # test whether an object is a legacy structural tag
6	    # see `StructuralTagResponseFormat` at `sglang.srt.entrypoints.openai.protocol`
7	    if obj.get("structures", None) is not None:
8	        assert obj.get("triggers", None) is not None
9	        return True
10	    else:
11	        assert obj.get("format", None) is not None
12	        return False

40	class GuidanceGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
41	
42	    def __init__(self, llguidance_tokenizer: LLTokenizer, serialized_grammar: str):
43	        super().__init__()
44	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = llguidance_tokenizer
45	        self.serialized_grammar = serialized_grammar
46	
47	        self.ll_matcher = LLMatcher(
48	            self.llguidance_tokenizer,
49	            self.serialized_grammar,
50	            log_level=int(os.environ.get("LLGUIDANCE_LOG_LEVEL", "1")),
51	        )
52	        self.bitmask = None
53	
54	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
55	        if not self.ll_matcher.consume_token(token):
56	            logger.warning(f"matcher error: {self.ll_matcher.get_error()}")
57	            self.finished = True
58	
59	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
60	        if self.ll_matcher.is_stopped():
61	            self.finished = True
62	
63	        fill_next_token_bitmask(self.ll_matcher, vocab_mask, idx)
64	
65	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
66	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
67	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
68	        if self.bitmask is None or self.bitmask.shape[0] < batch_size:
69	            # only create bitmask when batch gets larger
70	            self.bitmask = allocate_token_bitmask(
71	                batch_size, self.llguidance_tokenizer.vocab_size
72	            )
73	            bitmask = self.bitmask
74	        else:
75	            bitmask = self.bitmask[:batch_size]
76	
77	        return bitmask
78	
79	    @staticmethod
80	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
81	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
82	
83	    @staticmethod
84	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
85	        apply_token_bitmask_inplace(logits, vocab_mask)

87	    def copy(self):
88	        return GuidanceGrammar(
89	            llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
90	            serialized_grammar=self.serialized_grammar,
91	        )

94	        ff_tokens = self.ll_matcher.compute_ff_tokens()

96	            return ff_tokens, ""
97	        else:
98	            return None

105	    ):

111	    def __init__(
112	        self,
113	        tokenizer,
114	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
115	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
116	        n_vocab: Optional[int] = None,
117	    ):

120	        self.tokenizer = tokenizer

122	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
123	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = from_tokenizer(self.tokenizer, n_vocab)

125	    def _from_serialized(self, serialized_grammar) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
126	        try:
127	            return GuidanceGrammar(
128	                llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
129	                serialized_grammar=serialized_grammar,
130	            )
131	        except Exception as e:
132	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid grammar: {serialized_grammar=}, {e=}")
133	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
134	
135	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
136	        try:
137	            serialized_grammar = LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema(
138	                key_string,
139	                defaults={
140	                    "whitespace_flexible": self.any_whitespace,
141	                    "whitespace_pattern": self.whitespace_pattern,
142	                },
143	            )
144	        except Exception as e:
145	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
146	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
147	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
148	
149	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
150	        serialized_grammar = grammar_from("regex", key_string)
151	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
152	
153	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
154	        try:
155	            serialized_grammar = grammar_from("ebnf", key_string)
156	            return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
157	        except ValueError as e:
158	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
159	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ

161	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
162	        try:
163	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
164	            assert is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag)
165	            tags = [
166	                StructTag(
167	                    begin=structure["begin"],
168	                    grammar=structure["schema"],
169	                    end=structure["end"],
170	                    trigger=structural_tag["triggers"][0],  # TODO?
171	                )
172	                for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
173	            ]
174	            g = StructTag.to_grammar(tags)
175	            return self._from_serialized(g)
176	        except Exception as e:
177	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
178	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ

2113	                new_indices=keep_indices_device,
2114	                has_been_filtered=has_been_filtered,
2115	            )

2117	    def merge_batch(self, other: "ScheduleBatch"):
2118	        # NOTE: in spec v2 mode, we do not need wait verify here because
2119	        # 1) current batch is always prefill, whose seq_lens is not a future
2120	        # 2) other batch is always decode, which is finished in previous step
2121	
2122	        # Penalizer orchestrator must be merged before Batch.reqs is merged. This is because
2123	        # orchestrator.merge() depends on Batch.reqs during preparation of each penalizers, so it
2124	        # needs to be called with pre-merged Batch.reqs.
2125	        self.sampling_info.merge_batch(other.sampling_info)
2126

2128	        if self.model_config.is_encoder_decoder:
2129	            self.encoder_lens = torch.cat([self.encoder_lens, other.encoder_lens])
2130	            self.encoder_lens_cpu.extend(other.encoder_lens_cpu)
2131	        self.req_pool_indices = torch.cat(
2132	            [self.req_pool_indices, other.req_pool_indices]
2133	        )
2134	        self.seq_lens = torch.cat([self.seq_lens, other.seq_lens])
2135	        self.seq_lens_cpu = torch.cat([self.seq_lens_cpu, other.seq_lens_cpu])
2136	        self.orig_seq_lens = torch.cat([self.orig_seq_lens, other.orig_seq_lens])
2137	        self.out_cache_loc = None
2138	        self.seq_lens_sum += other.seq_lens_sum
2139	        if self.output_ids is not None:
2140	            self.output_ids = torch.cat([self.output_ids, other.output_ids])
2141	        self.mamba_track_indices = None
2142	        self.mamba_track_mask = None
2143	        self.mamba_track_seqlens = None
2144	        if self.return_logprob and other.return_logprob:
2145	            self.top_logprobs_nums.extend(other.top_logprobs_nums)
2146	            self.token_ids_logprobs.extend(other.token_ids_logprobs)
2147	        elif self.return_logprob:
2148	            self.top_logprobs_nums.extend([0] * len(other.reqs))
2149	            self.token_ids_logprobs.extend([None] * len(other.reqs))
2150	        elif other.return_logprob:
2151	            self.top_logprobs_nums = [0] * len(self.reqs) + other.top_logprobs_nums
2152	            self.token_ids_logprobs = [None] * len(self.reqs) + other.token_ids_logprobs
2153	        self.reqs.extend(other.reqs)
2154	        if self.multimodal_inputs is not None:
2155	            self.multimodal_inputs.extend(other.multimodal_inputs)
2156	
2157	        self.return_logprob |= other.return_logprob
2158	        self.has_stream |= other.has_stream
2159	        self.has_grammar |= other.has_grammar
2160	        self.return_hidden_states |= other.return_hidden_states
2161	
2162	        if self.spec_info:
2163	            self.spec_info.merge_batch(other.spec_info)

2167	    ) -> ModelWorkerBatch:
2168	        if self.forward_mode.is_decode_or_idle():
2169	            extend_seq_lens = extend_prefix_lens = extend_logprob_start_lens = None
2170	        else:
2171	            extend_seq_lens = self.extend_lens
2172	            extend_prefix_lens = self.prefix_lens
2173	            extend_logprob_start_lens = self.extend_logprob_start_lens
2174

2178	            else:
2179	                self.sampling_info.grammars = None

2181	        seq_lens_cpu = (
2182	            seq_lens_cpu_cache if seq_lens_cpu_cache is not None else self.seq_lens_cpu
2183	        )

27	class ReasonerGrammarObject(BaseGrammarObject):
28	    def __init__(self, grammar: BaseGrammarObject, think_end_id: int):
29	        super().__init__()
30	        self.grammar = grammar
31	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
32	        # -1    means thinking has not ended yet
33	        # 0     means just ended thinking in the last token
34	        # +     means number of tokens after thinking ended
35	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
36	
37	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
38	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1 if reasoning else 0
39	
40	    def transfer_state(self, token: int) -> int:
41	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == -1 and token == self.think_end_id:
42	            self.tokens_after_think_end = 0
43	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
44	            self.tokens_after_think_end += 1
45	
46	    def rollback_state(self):
47	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == 0:
48	            self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
49	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end > 0:
50	            self.tokens_after_think_end -= 1
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
53	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
54	            self.grammar.accept_token(token)
55	        self.transfer_state(token)
56	
57	    def is_terminated(self):
58	        return self.grammar.is_terminated()
59	
60	    def rollback(self, k):
61	        steps_after_think = min(k, self.tokens_after_think_end)
62	        if steps_after_think > 0:
63	            self.grammar.rollback(steps_after_think)
64	
65	        for _ in range(k):
66	            self.rollback_state()
67	
68	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
69	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
70	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
71	        return self.grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(vocab_size, batch_size, device)
72	
73	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
74	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
75	            self.grammar.fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, idx)
76	
77	    def move_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
78	        return self.grammar.move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, device)
79	
80	    @property
81	    def apply_vocab_mask(self):
82	        return self.grammar.apply_vocab_mask
83	
84	    def copy(self) -> BaseGrammarObject:
85	        return ReasonerGrammarObject(self.grammar.copy(), self.think_end_id)
86	
87	    @property
88	    def finished(self):
89	        return self.grammar.finished
90	
91	    @finished.setter

93	        self.grammar.finished = finished
94	
95	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer):
96	        return self.grammar.try_jump_forward(tokenizer)
97	
98	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper):
99	        return self.grammar.jump_forward_str_state(helper)
100	
101	    def jump_and_retokenize(
102	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
103	    ):
104	        return self.grammar.jump_and_retokenize(
105	            old_output_ids, new_output_ids, next_state
106	        )

109	class ReasonerGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):

111	        super().__init__()
112	        self.grammar_backend = grammar_backend
113	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
114	
115	    def _init_value_dispatch(
116	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], reasoning: bool
117	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
118	        ret = self.grammar_backend._init_value_dispatch(key, reasoning)
119	        # avoid wrapping invalid grammar, so that the scheduler can detect it
120	        if ret is None or ret is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:

123	        obj.maybe_init_reasoning(reasoning)

Perfect! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a final comprehensive summary with all the information gathered.

## Summary

I've conducted a thorough search of the SGLang codebase's output constraint system. Here's a comprehensive report of my findings:

### 1. **Existing Output Constraints Defined**

The following constraint types are supported in SGLang:

- **JSON Schema** (`json_schema`) - Constrains output to match a JSON schema
- **Regex** (`regex`) - Constrains output to match a regular expression pattern
- **EBNF** (`ebnf`) - Constrains output to match an EBNF grammar
- **Structural Tag** (`structural_tag`) - Legacy format for constraining specific output regions
- **Structural Pattern** (`structural_pattern`) - New format for flexible structural tagging

**Key Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503727070-pytt2s/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` - Defines `SamplingParams` class with constraint fields
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503727070-pytt2s/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` - OpenAI API integration with constraint support

### 2. **How Constraints Are Registered and Used**

**Registration Flow:**
1. User provides constraint via OpenAI API request (`response_format.json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf` parameters)
2. Constraint is converted to `SamplingParams` object in `CompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()` (protocol.py:699)
3. `Req` object is created with `sampling_params` containing the constraint
4. `GrammarManager` processes requests with constraints in `process_req_with_grammar()` (grammar_manager.py:67)

**Key Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503727070-pytt2s/python/sglang/srt/managers/schedule_batch.py` - `Req` class (line 512-1190) stores `grammar` and `grammar_key` fields
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503727070-pytt2s/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` - Manages constraint compilation and caching (lines 24-196)

### 3. **Base Class/Interface for Constraints**

**Abstract Base Classes:**

1. **`BaseGrammarObject`** (base_grammar_backend.py:42-118)
   - Core interface all constraint implementations inherit from
   - Methods:
     - `accept_token(token: int)` - Accept a token according to constraint
     - `fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, idx)` - Fill vocabulary mask for allowed tokens
     - `allocate_vocab_mask(vocab_size, batch_size, device)` - Allocate mask tensor
     - `apply_vocab_mask(logits, vocab_mask)` - Apply mask to logits
     - `move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, device)` - Move mask to device
     - `is_terminated()` - Check if grammar is complete
     - `rollback(k)` - Rollback k tokens
     - `copy()` - Create a copy for batch processing
     - `try_jump_forward(tokenizer)` - Optimization for fast forward
     - `jump_forward_str_state(helper)` - Jump forward state management

2. **`BaseGrammarBackend`** (base_grammar_backend.py:129-274)
   - Backend registry that dispatches constraints by type
   - Methods:
     - `dispatch_json(key_string)` - Compile JSON schema constraint
     - `dispatch_regex(key_string)` - Compile regex constraint
     - `dispatch_ebnf(key_string)` - Compile EBNF constraint
     - `dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)` - Compile structural tag

## Summary

I've conducted a thorough search of the SGLang codebase's output constraint system. Here's a comprehensive report of my findings:

### 1. **Existing Output Constraints Defined**

The following constraint types are supported in SGLang:

- **JSON Schema** (`json_schema`) - Constrains output to match a JSON schema
- **Regex** (`regex`) - Constrains output to match a regular expression pattern
- **EBNF** (`ebnf`) - Constrains output to match an EBNF grammar
- **Structural Tag** (`structural_tag`) - Legacy format for constraining specific output regions
- **Structural Pattern** (`structural_pattern`) - New format for flexible structural tagging

**Key Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503727070-pytt2s/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` - Defines `SamplingParams` class with constraint fields
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503727070-pytt2s/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` - OpenAI API integration with constraint support

### 2. **How Constraints Are Registered and Used**

**Registration Flow:**
1. User provides constraint via OpenAI API request (`response_format.json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf` parameters)
2. Constraint is converted to `SamplingParams` object in `CompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()` (protocol.py:699)
3. `Req` object is created with `sampling_params` containing the constraint
4. `GrammarManager` processes requests with constraints in `process_req_with_grammar()` (grammar_manager.py:67)

**Key Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503727070-pytt2s/python/sglang/srt/managers/schedule_batch.py` - `Req` class (line 512-1190) stores `grammar` and `grammar_key` fields
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503727070-pytt2s/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` - Manages constraint compilation and caching (lines 24-196)

### 3. **Base Class/Interface for Constraints**

**Abstract Base Classes:**

1. **`BaseGrammarObject`** (base_grammar_backend.py:42-118)
   - Core interface all constraint implementations inherit from
   - Methods:
     - `accept_token(token: int)` - Accept a token according to constraint
     - `fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, idx)` - Fill vocabulary mask for allowed tokens
     - `allocate_vocab_mask(vocab_size, batch_size, device)` - Allocate mask tensor
     - `apply_vocab_mask(logits, vocab_mask)` - Apply mask to logits
     - `move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, device)` - Move mask to device
     - `is_terminated()` - Check if grammar is complete
     - `rollback(k)` - Rollback k tokens
     - `copy()` - Create a copy for batch processing
     - `try_jump_forward(tokenizer)` - Optimization for fast forward
     - `jump_forward_str_state(helper)` - Jump forward state management

2. **`BaseGrammarBackend`** (base_grammar_backend.py:129-274)
   - Backend registry that dispatches constraints by type
   - Methods:
     - `dispatch_json(key_string)` - Compile JSON schema constraint
     - `dispatch_regex(key_string)` - Compile regex constraint
     - `dispatch_ebnf(key_string)` - Compile EBNF constraint
     - `dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)` - Compile structural tag
     - `get_cached_or_future_value(key, require_reasoning)` - Cache management
     - `set_cache(key, value)` - Cache constraint objects

### 4. **Constraint Wiring into Generation Pipeline**

**Pipeline Flow:**

```
Request → SamplingParams → Req.grammar
    ↓
GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar()
    ↓
Create grammar object via backend dispatcher
    ↓
ScheduleBatch.grammars = [req.grammar for req in reqs]
    ↓
SamplingBatchInfo.update_regex_vocab_mask()
    ↓
vocab_mask + apply_mask_func stored in SamplingBatchInfo
    ↓
SamplingBatchInfo.apply_logits_bias() applies mask during sampling
```

14	"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""

25	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
26	TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30

31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens

97	        # Process some special cases

102	        if self.top_k == -1:

105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:

112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(

141	                )

143	                raise ValueError(

145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:

152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154

156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,

160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:

163	    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
164	        # Process stop strings
165	        if self.stop_strs is None:
166	            self.stop_strs = []
167	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
168	        else:

176	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
177	                else:

179	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
180	
181	        # Process stop regex strings
182	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
183	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
184	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
187	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
188	
189	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
190	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
191	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
192	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
193	                )
194	
195	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len
196	
197	
198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
201	# token
202	def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
203	    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))
204	
205	
206	MAX_LEN = 2**30
207	
208	
209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total

# Copyright 2023-2024 SGLang Team
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
# ==============================================================================
"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""

import logging
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union

# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
try:
    import re._parser as sre_parse
except ImportError:
    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11

_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class SamplingParams:
    """
    The sampling parameters.

    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
    for the documentation.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        temperature: float = 1.0,
        top_p: float = 1.0,
        top_k: int = -1,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
        n: int = 1,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> None:
        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
        self.stop_strs = stop
        if stop_token_ids:
            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
        else:
            self.stop_token_ids = None
        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
        self.temperature = temperature
        self.top_p = top_p
        self.top_k = top_k
        self.min_p = min_p
        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
        self.regex = regex
        self.n = n
        self.json_schema = json_schema
        self.ebnf = ebnf
        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
        self.custom_params = custom_params
        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed

        # Process some special cases
        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
            self.temperature = 1.0
            self.top_k = 1
        if self.top_k == -1:
            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary

    def verify(self, vocab_size):
        if self.temperature < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
            )
        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
            )
        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
            )
        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
            )
        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
            )
        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
            raise ValueError(
                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
            )
        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
                )
            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
                )
        if self.logit_bias is not None:
            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
                    raise ValueError(
                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
                        f"{token_id}."
                    )

        grammars = [
            self.json_schema,
            self.regex,
            self.ebnf,
        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")

    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
        # Process stop strings
        if self.stop_strs is None:
            self.stop_strs = []
            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
        else:
            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]

            stop_str_max_len = 0
            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
                if tokenizer is not None:
                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
                else:
                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len

        # Process stop regex strings
        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
            self.stop_regex_strs = []
            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
        else:
            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]

            stop_regex_max_len = 0
            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
                stop_regex_max_len = max(
                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
                )

            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len


# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
# to be buffered to match the input regex string
# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
# token
def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))


MAX_LEN = 2**30


def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
    total = 0
    for token, value in subpattern:
        if token in {
            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
        }:
            total += 1
        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
            # EG: (a\d+) ->
            # [(SUBPATTERN,
            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
            _, branches = value
            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
                total += MAX_LEN
            else:
                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
            # length
            total += 0
        else:
            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")

            total += MAX_LEN

    return total

42	class BaseGrammarObject:
43	
44	    def __init__(self):
45	        self._finished = False
46	        self.grammar_stats = None
47	        self.current_token = None
48	
49	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
50	        pass
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
53	        """
54	        Accept a token in the grammar.
55	        """
56	        raise NotImplementedError()
57	
58	    def rollback(self, k: int):

65	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
66	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
67	        raise NotImplementedError()
68	
69	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
70	        raise NotImplementedError()
71	
72	    @staticmethod
73	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
74	        raise NotImplementedError()
75	
76	    @staticmethod
77	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
78	        raise NotImplementedError()

80	    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject":
81	        return self

84	    def finished(self):

86

91	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
92	        """

94	
95	        Returns:
96	            A jump forward helper which may be used in `jump_forward_str_state`.
97	            None if the jump forward is not possible.
98	        """

101	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
102	        """
103	        Jump forward for the grammar.
104	
105	        Returns:
106	            A tuple of the jump forward string and the next state of the grammar
107	            (which can be used in `jump_and_retokenize` if needed).
108	        """
109	        raise NotImplementedError()

111	    def jump_and_retokenize(
112	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
113	    ) -> None:

116	        """
117	        raise NotImplementedError()

120	INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ = BaseGrammarObject()
121	
122	
123	@dataclass
124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event
127	
128	
129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197	
198	
199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend
275

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import logging
4	import time
5	from concurrent import futures
6	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List
7	
8	import torch
9	
10	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
11	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
12	    create_grammar_backend,
13	)
14	from sglang.srt.environ import envs
15	
16	if TYPE_CHECKING:
17	    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
18	    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
19	    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler
20	
21	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22	
23	
24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import time
from concurrent import futures
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List

import torch

from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
    create_grammar_backend,
)
from sglang.srt.environ import envs

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class GrammarManager:
    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
        self.scheduler = scheduler
        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
                self.server_args,
                scheduler.tokenizer,
                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
            )
        else:
            self.grammar_backend = None

        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank

        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
        )

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.grammar_queue)

    def clear(self):
        if self.grammar_backend:
            self.grammar_backend.reset()

    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0

    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
        for req in self.grammar_queue:
            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
                if req.grammar:
                    req.grammar.cancel()
                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")

    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
        # Init grammar cache for this request
        add_to_grammar_queue = False
        if (
            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
        ):
            if self.grammar_backend is None:
                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
            else:
                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)

                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
                    key, req.require_reasoning
                )
                req.grammar = value

                if not cache_hit:
                    req.grammar_key = key
                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
                else:
                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        if add_to_grammar_queue:
            self.grammar_queue.append(req)

        return add_to_grammar_queue

    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
        """
        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.

        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)

        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
        """
        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()

        # Poll for ready requests
        start_time = time.perf_counter()
        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
                if i in ready_req_idxs:
                    continue

                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
                    continue

                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
                if req.grammar.done():
                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)

            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)

        # Check failed requests
        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
                if (
                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
                ):
                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)

        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
        else:
            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
                all_gather_output,
                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
            )
            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])

        # Return ready requests
        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
            return_reqs.append(req)
            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
                continue

            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        # Return failed requests
        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
            return_reqs.append(req)

            req.grammar.cancel()
            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
        self.grammar_queue = [
            req
            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
        ]
        return return_reqs

from openai.types.responses import (
    ResponseFunctionToolCall,
    ResponseInputItemParam,
    ResponseOutputItem,
    ResponseOutputMessage,
    ResponseOutputText,
    ResponseReasoningItem,
)

from pydantic import (
    BaseModel,
    Field,
    field_validator,
    model_serializer,
    model_validator,
)
from typing_extensions import Literal

try:
    from xgrammar import StructuralTag
except:
    StructuralTag = Any

from sglang.utils import convert_json_schema_to_str

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME = "default"

# SGLang public APIs

# Frontend Language APIs
from sglang.global_config import global_config
from sglang.lang.api import (
    Engine,
    Runtime,
    assistant,
    assistant_begin,
    assistant_end,
    flush_cache,
    function,
    gen,
    gen_int,
    gen_string,
    get_server_info,
    image,
    select,
    separate_reasoning,
    set_default_backend,
    system,
    system_begin,
    system_end,
    user,
    user_begin,
    user_end,
    video,
)
from sglang.lang.backend.runtime_endpoint import RuntimeEndpoint
from sglang.lang.choices import (
    greedy_token_selection,
    token_length_normalized,
    unconditional_likelihood_normalized,
)

# Lazy import some libraries
from sglang.utils import LazyImport
from sglang.version import __version__

Anthropic = LazyImport("sglang.lang.backend.anthropic", "Anthropic")
LiteLLM = LazyImport("sglang.lang.backend.litellm", "LiteLLM")
OpenAI = LazyImport("sglang.lang.backend.openai", "OpenAI")
VertexAI = LazyImport("sglang.lang.backend.vertexai", "VertexAI")

# Runtime Engine APIs
ServerArgs = LazyImport("sglang.srt.server_args", "ServerArgs")
Engine = LazyImport("sglang.srt.entrypoints.engine", "Engine")

__all__ = [
    "Engine",
    "Runtime",
    "assistant",
    "assistant_begin",
    "assistant_end",
    "flush_cache",
    "function",
    "gen",
    "gen_int",
    "gen_string",
    "get_server_info",
    "image",
    "select",
    "separate_reasoning",
    "set_default_backend",
    "system",
    "system_begin",
    "system_end",
    "user",
    "user_begin",
    "user_end",
    "video",
    "RuntimeEndpoint",
    "greedy_token_selection",
    "token_length_normalized",
    "unconditional_likelihood_normalized",
    "ServerArgs",
    "Anthropic",
    "LiteLLM",
    "OpenAI",
    "VertexAI",
    "global_config",
    "__version__",
]

705	        """
706	        Convert request to sampling parameters.
707	        Priority: user value > model generation_config > OpenAI defaults
708	        """
709	
710	        def get_param(param_name: str):
711	            value = getattr(self, param_name)
712	            if value is None:
713	                return model_generation_config.get(
714	                    param_name, self._DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS[param_name]
715	                )
716	            return value
717	
718	        # add per user request
719	        spaces_between_special_tokens = (
720	            True
721	            if self.chat_template_kwargs is None
722	            else self.chat_template_kwargs.get("spaces_between_special_tokens", True)
723	        )
724	
725	        sampling_params = {
726	            "temperature": get_param("temperature"),
727	            "max_new_tokens": self.max_completion_tokens or self.max_tokens,
728	            "min_new_tokens": self.min_tokens,
729	            "stop": stop,
730	            "stop_token_ids": self.stop_token_ids,
731	            "stop_regex": self.stop_regex,
732	            "top_p": get_param("top_p"),
733	            "top_k": get_param("top_k"),
734	            "min_p": get_param("min_p"),
735	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
736	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
737	            "repetition_penalty": get_param("repetition_penalty"),
738	            "regex": self.regex,
739	            "ebnf": self.ebnf,
740	            "n": self.n,
741	            "no_stop_trim": self.no_stop_trim,
742	            "ignore_eos": self.ignore_eos,
743	            "skip_special_tokens": self.skip_special_tokens,
744	            "logit_bias": self.logit_bias,
745	            "custom_params": self.custom_params,
746	            "sampling_seed": self.seed,
747	            "spaces_between_special_tokens": spaces_between_special_tokens,
748	        }
749	
750	        if self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "json_schema":
751	            sampling_params["json_schema"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
752	                self.response_format.json_schema.schema_
753	            )
754	        elif self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "json_object":
755	            sampling_params["json_schema"] = '{"type": "object"}'
756	        elif self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "structural_tag":
757	            sampling_params["structural_tag"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
758	                self.response_format.model_dump(by_alias=True)
759	            )
760	
761	        # Check if there are already existing output constraints
762	        has_existing_constraints = (
763	            sampling_params.get("regex")
764	            or sampling_params.get("ebnf")
765	            or sampling_params.get("structural_tag")
766	            or sampling_params.get("json_schema")
767	        )
768	
769	        if tool_call_constraint and has_existing_constraints:
770	            logger.warning("Constrained decoding is not compatible with tool calls.")
771	        elif tool_call_constraint:
772	            constraint_type, constraint_value = tool_call_constraint
773	            if constraint_type == "structural_tag":
774	                sampling_params[constraint_type] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
775	                    constraint_value.model_dump(by_alias=True)
776	                )
777	            elif constraint_type == "json_schema":
778	                sampling_params[constraint_type] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
779	                    constraint_value  # type: ignore
780	                )
781	            else:
782	                sampling_params[constraint_type] = constraint_value
783	
784	        return sampling_params

def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    n: Optional[int] = None,
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
):
    """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""

    if choices:
        return SglSelect(
            name,
            choices,
            0.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
            token_length_normalized if choices_method is None else choices_method,
        )

    # check regex is valid
    if regex is not None:
        try:
            re.compile(regex)
        except re.error as e:
            raise e

    return SglGen(
        name,
        max_tokens,
        min_tokens,
        n,
        stop,
        stop_token_ids,
        stop_regex,
        temperature,
        top_p,
        top_k,
        min_p,
        frequency_penalty,
        presence_penalty,
        ignore_eos,
        return_logprob,
        logprob_start_len,
        top_logprobs_num,
        return_text_in_logprobs,
        dtype,
        regex,
        json_schema,
    )


def gen_int(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,

"""The intermediate representation."""

import dataclasses
import inspect
import warnings
from typing import List, Optional, Union

from sglang.global_config import global_config
from sglang.lang.choices import ChoicesSamplingMethod

REGEX_INT = r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
REGEX_FLOAT = r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
REGEX_BOOL = r"(True|False)"
REGEX_STR = r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""  # bugs with regex r"\".*\"" in interegular pkg


@dataclasses.dataclass

    max_new_tokens: int = 128

    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = ()

class SglVideo(SglExpr):
    def __init__(self, path: str, num_frames: int):
        self.path = path
        self.num_frames = num_frames

    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        return f"SglVideo({self.path}, {self.num_frames})"


class SglGen(SglExpr):
    def __init__(
        self,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        max_new_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        min_new_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        top_k: Optional[int] = None,
        min_p: Optional[float] = None,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
        dtype: Optional[type] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""
        super().__init__()
        self.name = name
        self.sampling_params = SglSamplingParams(
            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
            min_new_tokens=min_new_tokens,
            n=n,
            stop=stop,
            stop_regex=stop_regex,
            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
            temperature=temperature,
            top_p=top_p,
            top_k=top_k,
            min_p=min_p,
            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
            return_logprob=return_logprob,
            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
            dtype=dtype,
            regex=regex,
            json_schema=json_schema,
        )

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"Gen('{self.name}')"


class SglConstantText(SglExpr):
    def __init__(self, value: str):
        super().__init__()
        self.value = value

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"Constant({repr(self.value)})"


class SglRoleBegin(SglExpr):
    def __init__(self, role: str):
        super().__init__()
        self.role = role

1171	        self, default_max_tokens: int, default_params: Optional[Dict] = None
1172	    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
1173	        """Convert to sampling parameters for generation."""
1174	        if default_params is None:
1175	            default_params = {}
1176	
1177	        # Use max_output_tokens if available, otherwise use max_tokens for backwards compatibility
1178	        if self.max_output_tokens is not None:
1179	            max_tokens = min(self.max_output_tokens, default_max_tokens)
1180	        else:
1181	            max_tokens = default_max_tokens
1182	
1183	        # Avoid exceed the context length by minus 2 token
1184	        max_tokens -= 2
1185	
1186	        # Get parameters with defaults
1187	        temperature = self.temperature
1188	        if temperature is None:
1189	            temperature = default_params.get(
1190	                "temperature", self._DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["temperature"]
1191	            )
1192

1195	            top_p = default_params.get("top_p", self._DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS["top_p"])
1196	
1197	        params = {
1198	            "max_new_tokens": max_tokens,
1199	            "temperature": temperature,
1200	            "top_p": top_p,

1202	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
1203	            "stop": self.stop,
1204	            "top_k": self.top_k,
1205	            "min_p": self.min_p,
1206	            "repetition_penalty": self.repetition_penalty,
1207	        }
1208

1214	        return params
